column4: And it came to pass on the eighth day, that {{c1::Moses}} called Aaron and his sons, and the elders of Israel;
And it came to pass on the eighth day, that Moses called {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons, and the elders of Israel;
and he said unto {{c1::Aaron}}: ‘Take thee a bull-calf for a sin-offering, and a ram for a burnt-offering, without blemish, and offer them before the LORD.
and he said unto Aaron: ‘{{c1::Take}} thee a bull-calf for a sin-offering, and a ram for a burnt-offering, without blemish, and offer them before the LORD.
And unto the children of {{c1::Israel}} thou shalt speak, saying: Take ye a he-goat for a sin-offering; and a calf and a lamb, both of the first year, without blemish, for a burnt-offering;
And unto the children of Israel thou shalt speak, saying: {{c1::Take}} ye a he-goat for a sin-offering; and a calf and a lamb, both of the first year, without blemish, for a burnt-offering;
and an ox and a ram for peace-{{c1::offerings}}, to sacrifice before the LORD; and a meal-offering mingled with oil; for to-day the LORD appeareth unto you.’
and an ox and a ram for peace-offerings, to {{c1::sacrifice}} before the LORD; and a meal-offering mingled with oil; for to-day the LORD appeareth unto you.’
And they brought that which {{c1::Moses}} commanded before the tent of meeting; and all the congregation drew near and stood before the LORD.
And they {{c1::brought}} that which Moses commanded before the tent of meeting; and all the congregation drew near and stood before the LORD.
And {{c1::Moses}} said: ‘This is the thing which the LORD commanded that ye should do; that the glory of the LORD may appear unto you.’
And Moses said: ‘{{c1::This}} is the thing which the LORD commanded that ye should do; that the glory of the LORD may appear unto you.’

And {{c1::Moses}} said unto Aaron: ‘Draw near unto the altar, and offer thy sin-offering, and thy burnt-offering, and make atonement for thyself, and for the people; and present the offering of the people, and make atonement for them; as the LORD commanded.’

And Moses said unto {{c1::Aaron}}: ‘Draw near unto the altar, and offer thy sin-offering, and thy burnt-offering, and make atonement for thyself, and for the people; and present the offering of the people, and make atonement for them; as the LORD commanded.’
So {{c1::Aaron}} drew near unto the altar, and slew the calf of the sin-offering, which was for himself.
So Aaron drew near unto the altar, and slew the calf of the sin-{{c1::offering}}, which was for himself.
And the sons of {{c1::Aaron}} presented the blood unto him; and he dipped his finger in the blood, and put it upon the horns of the altar, and poured out the blood at the base of the altar.
And the sons of Aaron {{c1::presented}} the blood unto him; and he dipped his finger in the blood, and put it upon the horns of the altar, and poured out the blood at the base of the altar.
But the fat, and the kidneys, and the lobe of the liver of the sin-offering, he made smoke upon the altar; as the LORD commanded {{c1::Moses}}.
But the fat, and the {{c1::kidneys}}, and the lobe of the liver of the sin-offering, he made smoke upon the altar; as the LORD commanded Moses.
And the flesh and the skin were burnt with fire {{c1::without}} the camp.
And he slew the burnt-offering; and {{c1::Aaron’s}} sons delivered unto him the blood, and he dashed it against the altar round about.
And he slew the burnt-{{c1::offering}}; and Aaron’s sons delivered unto him the blood, and he dashed it against the altar round about.
And they {{c1::delivered}} the burnt-offering unto him, piece by piece, and the head; and he made them smoke upon the altar.
And they delivered the burnt-{{c1::offering}} unto him, piece by piece, and the head; and he made them smoke upon the altar.
And he {{c1::washed}} the inwards and the legs, and made them smoke upon the burnt-offering on the altar.
And he washed the {{c1::inwards}} and the legs, and made them smoke upon the burnt-offering on the altar.
And the {{c1::people’s}} offering was presented; and he took the goat of the sin-offering which was for the people, and slew it, and offered it for sin, as the first.
And the people’s {{c1::offering}} was presented; and he took the goat of the sin-offering which was for the people, and slew it, and offered it for sin, as the first.
And the burnt-{{c1::offering}} was presented; and he offered it according to the ordinance.
And the burnt-offering was {{c1::presented}}; and he offered it according to the ordinance.
And the meal-{{c1::offering}} was presented; and he filled his hand therefrom, and made it smoke upon the altar, besides the burnt-offering of the morning.
And the meal-offering was {{c1::presented}}; and he filled his hand therefrom, and made it smoke upon the altar, besides the burnt-offering of the morning.
He slew also the ox and the ram, the sacrifice of peace-offerings, which was for the people; and {{c1::Aaron’s}} sons delivered unto him the blood, and he dashed it against the altar round about,
He slew also the ox and the ram, the {{c1::sacrifice}} of peace-offerings, which was for the people; and Aaron’s sons delivered unto him the blood, and he dashed it against the altar round about,
and the fat of the ox, and of the ram, the fat tail, and that which {{c1::covereth}} the inwards, and the kidneys, and the lobe of the liver.
and the fat of the ox, and of the ram, the fat tail, and that which covereth the {{c1::inwards}}, and the kidneys, and the lobe of the liver.
And they put the fat upon the {{c1::breasts}}, and he made the fat smoke upon the altar.
And the breasts and the right thigh {{c1::Aaron}} waved for a wave-offering before the LORD; as Moses commanded.
And the breasts and the right thigh Aaron waved for a wave-offering before the LORD; as {{c1::Moses}} commanded.
And {{c1::Aaron}} lifted up his hands toward the people, and blessed them; and he came down from offering the sin-offering, and the burnt-offering, and the peace-offerings.
And Aaron {{c1::lifted}} up his hands toward the people, and blessed them; and he came down from offering the sin-offering, and the burnt-offering, and the peace-offerings.
And {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron went into the tent of meeting, and came out, and blessed the people; and the glory of the LORD appeared unto all the people.
And Moses and {{c1::Aaron}} went into the tent of meeting, and came out, and blessed the people; and the glory of the LORD appeared unto all the people.
And there came forth fire from {{c1::before}} the LORD, and consumed upon the altar the burnt-offering and the fat; and when all the people saw it, they shouted, and fell on their faces.
And there came forth fire from before the LORD, and {{c1::consumed}} upon the altar the burnt-offering and the fat; and when all the people saw it, they shouted, and fell on their faces.

And {{c1::Nadab}} and Abihu, the sons of Aaron, took each of them his censer, and put fire therein, and laid incense thereon, and offered strange fire before the LORD, which He had not commanded them.

And Nadab and {{c1::Abihu}}, the sons of Aaron, took each of them his censer, and put fire therein, and laid incense thereon, and offered strange fire before the LORD, which He had not commanded them.
And there came forth fire from {{c1::before}} the LORD, and devoured them, and they died before the LORD.
And there came forth fire from before the LORD, and {{c1::devoured}} them, and they died before the LORD.

Then {{c1::Moses}} said unto Aaron: ‘This is it that the LORD spoke, saying: Through them that are nigh unto Me I will be sanctified, and before all the people I will be glorified.’ And Aaron held his peace.

Then Moses said unto {{c1::Aaron}}: ‘This is it that the LORD spoke, saying: Through them that are nigh unto Me I will be sanctified, and before all the people I will be glorified.’ And Aaron held his peace.
And {{c1::Moses}} called Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said unto them: ‘Draw near, carry your brethren from before the sanctuary out of the camp.’
And Moses called {{c1::Mishael}} and Elzaphan, the sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said unto them: ‘Draw near, carry your brethren from before the sanctuary out of the camp.’
So they drew near, and carried them in their tunics out of the camp, as {{c1::Moses}} had said.
So they drew near, and {{c1::carried}} them in their tunics out of the camp, as Moses had said.

And {{c1::Moses}} said unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons: ‘Let not the hair of your heads go loose, neither rend your clothes, that ye die not, and that He be not wroth with all the congregation; but let your brethren, the whole house of Israel, bewail the burning which the LORD hath kindled.

And Moses said unto {{c1::Aaron}}, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons: ‘Let not the hair of your heads go loose, neither rend your clothes, that ye die not, and that He be not wroth with all the congregation; but let your brethren, the whole house of Israel, bewail the burning which the LORD hath kindled.
And ye shall not go out from the door of the tent of meeting, lest ye die; for the anointing oil of the LORD is upon you.’ And they did according to the word of {{c1::Moses}}.
And ye shall not go out from the door of the tent of {{c1::meeting}}, lest ye die; for the anointing oil of the LORD is upon you.’ And they did according to the word of Moses.
And the LORD spoke unto {{c1::Aaron}}, saying:
And the LORD spoke unto Aaron, {{c1::saying}}:
’Drink no wine nor strong drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tent of meeting, that ye die not; it shall be a statute forever throughout your generations.
’Drink no wine nor {{c1::strong}} drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tent of meeting, that ye die not; it shall be a statute forever throughout your generations.
And that ye may put {{c1::difference}} between the holy and the common, and between the unclean and the clean;
And that ye may put difference {{c1::between}} the holy and the common, and between the unclean and the clean;
and that ye may teach the children of {{c1::Israel}} all the statutes which the LORD hath spoken unto them by the hand of Moses.’
and that ye may teach the children of Israel all the statutes which the LORD hath spoken unto them by the hand of {{c1::Moses}}.’

And {{c1::Moses}} spoke unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons that were left: ‘Take the meal-offering that remaineth of the offerings of the LORD made by fire, and eat it without leaven beside the altar; for it is most holy.

And Moses spoke unto {{c1::Aaron}}, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons that were left: ‘Take the meal-offering that remaineth of the offerings of the LORD made by fire, and eat it without leaven beside the altar; for it is most holy.
And ye shall eat it in a holy place, because it is thy due, and thy sons’due, of the offerings of the LORD made by fire; for so {{c1::I}} am commanded.
And ye shall eat it in a holy place, {{c1::because}} it is thy due, and thy sons’due, of the offerings of the LORD made by fire; for so I am commanded.

And the breast of waving and the thigh of heaving shall ye eat in a clean place; thou, and thy sons, and thy daughters with thee; for they are given as thy due, and thy sons’due, out of the sacrifices of the peace-offerings of the children of {{c1::Israel}}.

And the {{c1::breast}} of waving and the thigh of heaving shall ye eat in a clean place; thou, and thy sons, and thy daughters with thee; for they are given as thy due, and thy sons’due, out of the sacrifices of the peace-offerings of the children of Israel.

The thigh of {{c1::heaving}} and the breast of waving shall they bring with the offerings of the fat made by fire, to wave it for a wave-offering before the LORD; and it shall be thine, and thy sons’with thee, as a due for ever; as the LORD hath commanded.’

The thigh of heaving and the {{c1::breast}} of waving shall they bring with the offerings of the fat made by fire, to wave it for a wave-offering before the LORD; and it shall be thine, and thy sons’with thee, as a due for ever; as the LORD hath commanded.’
And {{c1::Moses}} diligently inquired for the goat of the sin-offering, and, behold, it was burnt; and he was angry with Eleazar and with Ithamar, the sons of Aaron that were left, saying:
And Moses diligently inquired for the goat of the sin-offering, and, behold, it was burnt; and he was angry with {{c1::Eleazar}} and with Ithamar, the sons of Aaron that were left, saying:

’Wherefore have ye not eaten the sin-offering in the place of the sanctuary, seeing it is most holy, and {{c1::He}} hath given it you to bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD?

’Wherefore have ye not eaten the sin-offering in the place of the sanctuary, seeing it is most holy, and He hath given it you to bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD?
Behold, the blood of it was not brought into the sanctuary within; ye should certainly have eaten it in the sanctuary, as {{c1::I}} commanded.’
{{c1::Behold}}, the blood of it was not brought into the sanctuary within; ye should certainly have eaten it in the sanctuary, as I commanded.’

And {{c1::Aaron}} spoke unto Moses: ‘Behold, this day have they offered their sin-offering and their burnt-offering before the LORD, and there have befallen me such things as these; and if I had eaten the sin-offering to-day, would it have been well-pleasing in the sight of the LORD?

And Aaron spoke unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Behold, this day have they offered their sin-offering and their burnt-offering before the LORD, and there have befallen me such things as these; and if I had eaten the sin-offering to-day, would it have been well-pleasing in the sight of the LORD?
And when {{c1::Moses}} heard that, it was well-pleasing in his sight.
And when Moses heard that, it was well-{{c1::pleasing}} in his sight.
And the LORD spoke unto {{c1::Moses}} and to Aaron, saying unto them:
And the LORD spoke unto Moses and to {{c1::Aaron}}, saying unto them:
Speak unto the children of {{c1::Israel}}, saying: These are the living things which ye may eat among all the beasts that are on the earth.
Speak unto the children of Israel, saying: {{c1::These}} are the living things which ye may eat among all the beasts that are on the earth.
{{c1::Whatsoever}} parteth the hoof, and is wholly cloven-footed, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that may ye eat.
Whatsoever {{c1::parteth}} the hoof, and is wholly cloven-footed, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that may ye eat.
{{c1::Nevertheless}} these shall ye not eat of them that only chew the cud, or of them that only part the hoof: the camel, because he cheweth the cud but parteth not the hoof, he is unclean unto you.
Nevertheless these shall ye not eat of them that only chew the cud, or of them that only part the hoof: the camel, {{c1::because}} he cheweth the cud but parteth not the hoof, he is unclean unto you.
And the rock-{{c1::badger}}, because he cheweth the cud but parteth not the hoof, he is unclean unto you.
And the rock-badger, {{c1::because}} he cheweth the cud but parteth not the hoof, he is unclean unto you.
And the hare, {{c1::because}} she cheweth the cud but parteth not the hoof, she is unclean unto you.
And the hare, because she {{c1::cheweth}} the cud but parteth not the hoof, she is unclean unto you.
And the swine, {{c1::because}} he parteth the hoof, and is cloven-footed, but cheweth not the cud, he is unclean unto you.
And the swine, because he {{c1::parteth}} the hoof, and is cloven-footed, but cheweth not the cud, he is unclean unto you.
Of their flesh ye shall not eat, and their {{c1::carcasses}} ye shall not touch; they are unclean unto you.
Of their flesh ye shall not eat, and their carcasses ye shall not touch; they are {{c1::unclean}} unto you.
These may ye eat of all that are in the {{c1::waters}}: whatsoever hath fins and scales in the waters, in the seas, and in the rivers, them may ye eat.
These may ye eat of all that are in the waters: {{c1::whatsoever}} hath fins and scales in the waters, in the seas, and in the rivers, them may ye eat.

And all that have not fins and {{c1::scales}} in the seas, and in the rivers, of all that swarm in the waters, and of all the living creatures that are in the waters, they are a detestable thing unto you,

And all that have not fins and scales in the seas, and in the {{c1::rivers}}, of all that swarm in the waters, and of all the living creatures that are in the waters, they are a detestable thing unto you,
and they shall be a {{c1::detestable}} thing unto you; ye shall not eat of their flesh, and their carcasses ye shall have in detestation.
and they shall be a detestable thing unto you; ye shall not eat of their flesh, and their {{c1::carcasses}} ye shall have in detestation.
{{c1::Whatsoever}} hath no fins nor scales in the waters, that is a detestable thing unto you.
Whatsoever hath no fins nor {{c1::scales}} in the waters, that is a detestable thing unto you.
And these ye shall have in {{c1::detestation}} among the fowls; they shall not be eaten, they are a detestable thing: the great vulture, and the bearded vulture, and the ospray;
And these ye shall have in detestation among the fowls; they shall not be eaten, they are a {{c1::detestable}} thing: the great vulture, and the bearded vulture, and the ospray;
and the kite, and the {{c1::falcon}} after its kinds;
and the {{c1::ostrich}}, and the night-hawk, and the sea-mew, and the hawk after its kinds;
and the {{c1::little}} owl, and the cormorant, and the great owl;
and the little owl, and the {{c1::cormorant}}, and the great owl;
and the {{c1::horned}} owl, and the pelican, and the carrion-vulture;
and the horned owl, and the {{c1::pelican}}, and the carrion-vulture;
and the stork, and the heron after its kinds, and the {{c1::hoopoe}}, and the bat.
All {{c1::winged}} swarming things that go upon all fours are a detestable thing unto you.
All winged {{c1::swarming}} things that go upon all fours are a detestable thing unto you.
Yet these may ye eat of all {{c1::winged}} swarming things that go upon all fours, which have jointed legs above their feet, wherewith to leap upon the earth;
Yet these may ye eat of all winged {{c1::swarming}} things that go upon all fours, which have jointed legs above their feet, wherewith to leap upon the earth;
even these of them ye may eat: the {{c1::locust}} after its kinds, and the bald locust after its kinds, and the cricket after its kinds, and the grasshopper after its kinds.
even these of them ye may eat: the locust after its kinds, and the bald locust after its kinds, and the {{c1::cricket}} after its kinds, and the grasshopper after its kinds.
But all {{c1::winged}} swarming things, which have four feet, are a detestable thing unto you.
But all winged {{c1::swarming}} things, which have four feet, are a detestable thing unto you.
And by these ye shall {{c1::become}} unclean; whosoever toucheth the carcass of them shall be unclean until even.
And by these ye shall become {{c1::unclean}}; whosoever toucheth the carcass of them shall be unclean until even.
And {{c1::whosoever}} beareth aught of the carcass of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
And whosoever {{c1::beareth}} aught of the carcass of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
Every beast which {{c1::parteth}} the hoof, but is not cloven footed, nor cheweth the cud, is unclean unto you; every one that to toucheth them shall be unclean.
Every beast which parteth the hoof, but is not {{c1::cloven}} footed, nor cheweth the cud, is unclean unto you; every one that to toucheth them shall be unclean.
And {{c1::whatsoever}} goeth upon its paws, among all beasts that go on all fours, they are unclean unto you; whoso toucheth their carcass shall be unclean until the even.
And whatsoever goeth upon its paws, among all {{c1::beasts}} that go on all fours, they are unclean unto you; whoso toucheth their carcass shall be unclean until the even.
And he that {{c1::beareth}} the carcass of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even; they are unclean unto you.
And he that beareth the {{c1::carcass}} of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even; they are unclean unto you.
And these are they which are {{c1::unclean}} unto you among the swarming things that swarm upon the earth: the weasel, and the mouse, and the great lizard after its kinds,
And these are they which are unclean unto you among the {{c1::swarming}} things that swarm upon the earth: the weasel, and the mouse, and the great lizard after its kinds,
and the gecko, and the land-{{c1::crocodile}}, and the lizard, and the sand-lizard, and the chameleon.
and the gecko, and the land-crocodile, and the {{c1::lizard}}, and the sand-lizard, and the chameleon.
These are they which are {{c1::unclean}} to you among all that swarm; whosoever doth touch them, when they are dead, shall be unclean until the even.
These are they which are unclean to you among all that swarm; {{c1::whosoever}} doth touch them, when they are dead, shall be unclean until the even.

And upon {{c1::whatsoever}} any of them, when they are dead, doth fall, it shall be unclean; whether it be any vessel of wood, or raiment, or skin, or sack, whatsoever vessel it be, wherewith any work is done, it must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the even; then shall it be clean.

And upon whatsoever any of them, when they are dead, doth fall, it shall be {{c1::unclean}}; whether it be any vessel of wood, or raiment, or skin, or sack, whatsoever vessel it be, wherewith any work is done, it must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the even; then shall it be clean.
And every {{c1::earthen}} vessel whereinto any of them falleth, whatsoever is in it shall be unclean, and it ye shall break.
And every earthen {{c1::vessel}} whereinto any of them falleth, whatsoever is in it shall be unclean, and it ye shall break.
All food {{c1::therein}} which may be eaten, that on which water cometh, shall be unclean; and all drink in every such vessel that may be drunk shall be unclean.
All food therein which may be eaten, that on which water {{c1::cometh}}, shall be unclean; and all drink in every such vessel that may be drunk shall be unclean.
And every thing {{c1::whereupon}} any part of their carcass falleth shall be unclean; whether oven, or range for pots, it shall be broken in pieces; they are unclean, and shall be unclean unto you.
And every thing whereupon any part of their {{c1::carcass}} falleth shall be unclean; whether oven, or range for pots, it shall be broken in pieces; they are unclean, and shall be unclean unto you.
{{c1::Nevertheless}} a fountain or a cistern wherein is a gathering of water shall be clean; but he who toucheth their carcass shall be unclean.
Nevertheless a {{c1::fountain}} or a cistern wherein is a gathering of water shall be clean; but he who toucheth their carcass shall be unclean.
And if aught of their {{c1::carcass}} fall upon any sowing seed which is to be sown, it is clean.
And if aught of their carcass fall upon any {{c1::sowing}} seed which is to be sown, it is clean.
But if water be put upon the seed, and aught of their {{c1::carcass}} fall thereon, it is unclean unto you.
But if water be put upon the seed, and aught of their carcass fall {{c1::thereon}}, it is unclean unto you.
And if any beast, of which ye may eat, die, he that {{c1::toucheth}} the carcass thereof shall be unclean until the even.
And if any beast, of which ye may eat, die, he that toucheth the {{c1::carcass}} thereof shall be unclean until the even.
And he that {{c1::eateth}} of the carcass of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even; he also that beareth the carcass of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
And he that eateth of the {{c1::carcass}} of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even; he also that beareth the carcass of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
And every {{c1::swarming}} thing that swarmeth upon the earth is a detestable thing; it shall not be eaten.
And every swarming thing that {{c1::swarmeth}} upon the earth is a detestable thing; it shall not be eaten.

{{c1::Whatsoever}} goeth upon the belly, and whatsoever goeth upon all fours, or whatsoever hath many feet, even all swarming things that swarm upon the earth, them ye shall not eat; for they are a detestable thing.

Whatsoever goeth upon the belly, and whatsoever goeth upon all fours, or whatsoever hath many feet, even all {{c1::swarming}} things that swarm upon the earth, them ye shall not eat; for they are a detestable thing.
Ye shall not make {{c1::yourselves}} detestable with any swarming thing that swarmeth, neither shall ye make yourselves unclean with them, that ye should be defiled thereby.
Ye shall not make yourselves {{c1::detestable}} with any swarming thing that swarmeth, neither shall ye make yourselves unclean with them, that ye should be defiled thereby.
For {{c1::I}} am the LORD your God; sanctify yourselves therefore, and be ye holy; for I am holy; neither shall ye defile yourselves with any manner of swarming thing that moveth upon the earth.
For I am the LORD your God; {{c1::sanctify}} yourselves therefore, and be ye holy; for I am holy; neither shall ye defile yourselves with any manner of swarming thing that moveth upon the earth.
For {{c1::I}} am the LORD that brought you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God; ye shall therefore be holy, for I am holy. .
For I am the LORD that brought you up out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, to be your God; ye shall therefore be holy, for I am holy. .
This is the law of the beast, and of the fowl, and of every {{c1::living}} creature that moveth in the waters, and of every creature that swarmeth upon the earth;
This is the law of the beast, and of the fowl, and of every living {{c1::creature}} that moveth in the waters, and of every creature that swarmeth upon the earth;
to make a {{c1::difference}} between the unclean and the clean, and between the living thing that may be eaten and the living thing that may not be eaten.
to make a difference {{c1::between}} the unclean and the clean, and between the living thing that may be eaten and the living thing that may not be eaten.
